files.year_idx_format: int32_t max_okey (4-byte header) followed by uint8_t[max_okey+1]; value = (year-1992) for valid keys (0-6), 0xFF for unused

storage_extensions.usage: mmap year_idx.bin; read int32_t max_okey from first 4 bytes; okey_year_arr = (const uint8_t*)(mmap_ptr + 4); then year_idx = okey_year_arr[l_orderkey] (no hash probe, no JDN conversion needed). Replaces o_orderkey_hash entirely.

join_order.notes: Parallel scan of p_name.bin (112MB, stride=56) across all threads, each thread handles its chunk. Build shared is_green[2000001] bool bitset (250KB, fits in L2). Rejects 94.5% of lineitem rows.

After is_green is built, scan raw partsupp columns in parallel (ps_partkey.bin 32MB + ps_suppkey.bin 32MB + ps_supplycost.bin 64MB = 128MB). Keep only rows where is_green[ps_partkey]. Build compact runtime hash: capacity 1048576 (2^20), key=int64((ps_partkey<<32)|ps_suppkey), value=double ps_supplycost. Size = 1M slots x 16 bytes = 16MB, fits entirely in L3 (44MB). Load factor ~44K/1M = 0.42. Replaces 201MB ps_composite_hash.
Build uint8_t supp_nat[100001] from s_suppkey.bin + s_nationkey.bin. Direct indexed lookup, 100KB, L1 cache resident.

Use okey_year_arr from storage extension. Direct uint8_t lookup, no hash probing, no JDN calculation. Max key 60M so array is 60MB but only ~825K distinct keys accessed by qualifying rows (35MB of unique cache lines, fits in 44MB L3 after warmup).
char nation_names[25][26] from n_name.bin, 650 bytes, L1 cache.

scan.notes: Only l_partkey is touched for 94.5% of rows (rejected by is_green filter). l_suppkey, l_orderkey, l_extprice, l_discount, l_quantity only accessed for qualifying ~5.5% of rows. Do NOT mmap_prefetch payload columns; rely on hardware prefetch and kernel readahead.

data_structures.part_filter: uint8_t is_green[2000001] — 2MB bool array indexed by p_partkey, built by parallel p_name scan; shared read-only across threads
data_structures.compact_ps_hash: runtime flat hash: uint64_t keys[1048576] (sentinel=0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF), double values[1048576]; key=(int64(ps_partkey)<<32|uint32(ps_suppkey)); 16MB total, L3-resident; built in parallel from raw partsupp columns after is_green is ready
data_structures.supplier_lookup: uint8_t supp_nat[100001] direct array indexed by s_suppkey, 100KB, L1-resident
data_structures.orders_lookup: uint8_t okey_year_arr[max_okey+1] from storage extension mmap; direct index by l_orderkey; 60MB but ~35MB unique cache lines accessed by qualifying rows (fits in 44MB L3)
data_structures.nation_lookup: char nation_names[25][26] from n_name.bin; 650 bytes, L1-resident
data_structures.aggregation: double thread_agg[25][7] per thread (1400 bytes, L1-resident); year_idx = okey_year_arr[l_orderkey]; nation_idx = supp_nat[l_suppkey]

parallelism.notes: Phase 1 (setup): use nthreads for parallel p_name scan and parallel partsupp scan. Phase 2 (lineitem scan): morsel-driven over 60M rows. Thread-local agg[25][7] avoids synchronization. Reduce after all morsels complete.

optimization_notes: Iter 0 showed data_loading=182ms (of which main_scan=58ms), leaving 124ms of single-threaded setup and 58ms of parallel scan — both need improvement to reach the 38ms target. Root cause analysis: (1) Q2/physical mismatch — the ps_composite_hash (201MB) and o_orderkey_hash (268MB) both greatly exceed the 44MB L3, causing frequent LLC misses on every qualifying row probe; (2) Q1/logical mismatch — setup work (p_name scan, partsupp scan) runs single-threaded when it could use all 64 cores. Strategy is Category B (algorithm change) plus Category E (storage extension). For orders: built a uint8_t direct-indexed array (okey_year_arr[orderkey] = year_idx) stored as a column_version extension; this replaces the 268MB hash probe with a single direct array access, and the working set of distinct cache lines accessed by the ~825K qualifying orders is ~35MB — just under the 44MB L3. For partsupp: instead of probing the pre-built 201MB composite hash, we scan the raw partsupp columns in parallel (128MB sequential) and build a compact runtime hash of only the ~440K green qualifying entries (16MB — fully L3-resident). This shrinks the partsupp lookup working set from 201MB to 16MB. All setup phases (p_name scan + partsupp scan) are parallelized across all threads. The o_orderkey_hash and ps_composite_hash are no longer used.
